Cognate with Odia ବିଜୁଳି (bijuḷi), Gujarati વીજળી (vījaḷī), Nepali बिजुली (bijulī), Assamese বিজুলী (bizuli), Punjabi ਬਿਜਲੀ (bijlī), Sindhi بجلي (bjle).

Cognate with Odia ବିଜୁଳି (bijuḷi), Gujarati વીજળી (vījaḷī), Nepali बिजुली (bijulī), Assamese বিজুলী (bizuli), Punjabi ਬਿਜਲੀ (bijlī), Sindhi بجلي (bjle).

s.m. Disc of the sun or moon; reflected form, reflection, shadow, image, picture; a plant bearing a bright-red gourd, Momordica monadelpha; the fruit of this plant.

def: part. adj. Seeing, having sight; clear-sighted, discerning
subs: v.t. To cause to see, give sight to; to impart the faculty of discernment to (-ko).

def: prep. See s.v. binā, 'foundation.' s.f. Building, structure, edifice; foundation, basis, base; ground, footing, motive; root, source, origin; beginning, commencement

def: s.m. Picker or gatherer of cotton.
s.f. Eye-sight, sight, vision.
s.f. Weaving, knitting; — price paid for weaving, &c. ( = bunwāʼī).

ety: S. विनाश; rt. नश् with वि
def: s.m. Disappearance, destruction, utter loss, ruin, death, annihilation
subs: v.t. To wipe out, efface, ruin. spoil, destroy, annihilate.

def: s.m. = binās, q.v. s.m. Disappearance, destruction, utter loss, ruin, death, annihilation
subs: v.t. To wipe out, efface, ruin. spoil, destroy, annihilate.

def: v.t. To ruin, destroy, &c. = binās karnā, q.v.s.v. binās. s.m. Disappearance, destruction, utter loss, ruin, death, annihilation
subs: v.t. To wipe out, efface, ruin. spoil, destroy, annihilate.

def: v.t. = bināsnā; binās karnā, q.q.v. v.t. To ruin, destroy, &c. = binās karnā, q.v.s.v. binās. s.m. Disappearance, destruction, utter loss, ruin, death, annihilation
subs: v.t. To wipe out, efface, ruin. spoil, destroy, annihilate.

def: v.n. To deteriorate, spoil, perish, die; to be brought to nothing, to be destroyed, be killed.
v.n. = binasnā, q.v. v.t. To ruin, destroy, &c. = binās karnā, q.v.s.v. binās. s.m. Disappearance, destruction, utter loss, ruin, death, annihilation
subs: v.t. To wipe out, efface, ruin. spoil, destroy, annihilate.

def: part. Sunk down, drooping, inclined, bowed, bent, curved, crooked, stooping; cast down, prostrate, humble, modest; — s.f. Bending or bowing low, &c. = bintī, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic بِنْت (bint).

def: s.f. Bowing down, bending low; humility, modesty; submission; apology; solicitation, entreaty, supplication, petition
subs: (-kī), To entreat (of), beseech, implore, supplicate, &c.; — bintī-kar, adv. Supplicatingly, beseechingly.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit विज्ञप्ति (vijñapti).

def: s.m. Cotton-seed (considered very fattening food for cattle: it is also burnt with oil as a light)
s.f. Cotton-seed; — small hail-stones.
v.t. To adore, revere, venerate, laud (syn. arćnā; pūjā karnā).
s.m. = binaulā, q.v. s.m. Cotton-seed (considered very fattening food for cattle: it is also burnt with oil as a light)
s.f. = binaulī, q.v. s.f. Cotton-seed; — small hail-stones.
s.f. Name of a herb which grows about a foot and a half high in fields which have been sown with a ḵẖarīf crop (it is used as fodder for cattle).
s.f. Weaving; knitting; texture.
wety: From بَنْنَا (bannā, “to make”) + ـَاوَٹ (-āvaṭ).

def: s.m. Supplication, entreaty; humility; modesty; graciousness, courtesy, good-nature; discretion, decorum; reverence, obeisance.
s.m. A spiritual preceptor; a name of the god Ganeśa; name of Garuḍ; name of a Bauddha.
s.m. &c. = bīć, q.v.
s.m. Reed; rush (cf. beṅdhnā).
s.f. Heap, bundle (as of straw, rushes, &c.), load (of straw, &c.), truss (of hay).
s.m. Faggot; bundle of sticks, or of grass, or of straw, &c.
s.m. = bīṅḍā, q.v. s.m. A round sectarial spot made on the forehead by Hindūs preparatory to worship.
s.m. Bundle, &c. ( = bīṅḍ); roll (of paper, &c.); twist of grass or fibre of a plant (used as a substitute for a rope).
s.m. A round sectarial spot made on the forehead by Hindūs preparatory to worship.
s.m. = bindrā-ban, br̤indā-ban, qq.v.
s.f. A medicinal herb.
s.m. A sort of curry made of pork.
s.m. Piercing, boring; perforation, hole.
s.m. The range of mountains which stretches across India, and divides Hindūstān proper from the Dakkhan, marking the southern boundary of the sacred land of the Hindūs.
v.t. To pierce, perforate, bore, make a hole through or in: to string (pearls, beads, &c.); to pierce or wound with sharp speech, to say cutting things to.
v.t. To puncture, &c. = bīṅdhnā, q.v.; — v.n. (from bīṅdhṉā), To be punctured, be perforated, bored, or pierced; to be stung.
v.t. To cause to be pierced, to have or get pierced, bored, &c.; to pierce, perforate, bore, &c. ( = bīṅdhnā, q.v.).
s.m. = bindhāćal, q.v. s.m. The range of mountains which stretches across India, and divides Hindūstān proper from the Dakkhan, marking the southern boundary of the sacred land of the Hindūs.
s.f. Dot over a letter; dot, point, spot, mark, cypher, nought; — spangle, ornament for the forehead.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بندی (bandī).

def: s.f. The hair twisted or plaited behind; braided hair; queuc, tail
subs: v.t. To form the hair into plaits or braids.

def: s.m. A third ox yoked before a pair of draught oxen, or a fifth yoked before two pairs.
s.m. Drop; point, dot, spot. mark; the nasal dot anusvāra; semen, sperma genitale
subs: s.m. Semen cito emittens morbo affectus.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَنْد (band).

def: s.f. Nose, snout; — the part of a door-leaf which overlaps the other; rib of wood fixed on a door-panel to strengthen it; the guard of a sword
subs: adj. Having the nose cut, nose-clipt, noseless ( = nakṭā).

def: s.f. The act of seeing or regarding (used in comp.)
s.f. Seeing, sight; discernment.
part. adj. Well-trained, educated, disciplined, well-behaved, well-controlled; compliant, governable, tractable, humble, modest, meek, placid, gentle.
s.m. = bīj, q.v.

s.m. Anything used in dressing or preparing food, seasoning, relish, sauce, condiment; vegetables (dressed with butter, or ghī, and added to flesh or fish); — (in Gram.) a consonant (as marking or distinguishing sound).
v.t. See بننا binnā.
ex: انسان پانی کے بنا جی نہیں سکتے
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀺𑀡𑀸 (viṇā), from Sanskrit विना (vínā). Doublet of وِنَا (vinā), a tatsama. Cognate with Punjabi ਬਿਨ (bin) / بِن (bin), Mewari वना (vanā) Kachchi વિણ- (viṇa-), Marathi बिन- (bin-).

def: v.t. See بيننا bīnnā.

ety: bīn˚, or bin˚ = Prk. वीण(इ), or विण(इ) = S. व्रीणा(ति), or व्रिणा(ति), rt. व्री
def: v.t. To pick, glean, gather, cull, pluck; to pick out, select.
v.t. To pick up, gather up, &c. ( = bīnnā, q.v.); — v.n. To be picked, culled; to be cleaned.

ety: bin˚ or bun˚ = Prk. विण(इ) or वुण(इ) = S. वृणा(ति) or वृणो(ति), rt. वृ
def: v.t. To weave; to braid, plait, knit; to intertwine, interlace.
s.m. Dismissing removing; dismissal, abandonment; diversion, sport, pastime, play, entertainment, pleasure, gratification, happiness, interest; eagerness, vehemence.
s.m. A small roll of cloth, &c. (see bīṅḍā, and īṅḍwā).
s.m. = bhinsār, q.v. s.m. Dawn of day, day-break, day-light, early morning (also written bhinusār, bhinnsār); — adv. At dawn, &c.

ety: fem. of ابن ibn; rt. بني
def: s.f. Daughter
subs: s.f. Daughter of the grape, wine.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic بِنْت (bint).

def: s.f. Cost of, or price paid for, weaving, &c.
v.t. To cause to be picked, to have or get (a thing) picked, &c.

ety: caus. of binnā or bunnā 'to weave'
def: v.t. To cause to be woven, &c.; to get (a thing) woven, &c.
Moment, instant, second.
s.f. Opposition, contrariety, contrary declaration; hostility, disaffection, mischief, ruin
subs: To reverse an order or decision
To misrepresent; to make a false statement
(-ke), To pervert; to turn away or aside, to be hostile; to violate

def: v.n. = bipharnā, q.v. v.n. To struggle (against, -se), resist; to disagree, jar, clash; to become vexed, irritated or enraged (with, -se), to break into a passion (syn. ćiṛnā); to break loose from control. to revolt, rise (against), make an attack (upon); to turn wild and wanton, run riot; to get into disorder, run to ruin; to be perverse, refractory. obstinate, &c. ( = ḍhīṭh honā); to insist, persist (in).
s.m. Adversity, calamity, misfortune, trouble, disaster, mishap; distress, pain, suffering
subs: (-par), Calamity or misfortune befalling, to be overtaken by calamity, &c.

def: s.m. Thursday ( = bihphai, q.v.). s.m. Thursday.

v.n. To struggle (against, -se), resist; to disagree, jar, clash; to become vexed, irritated or enraged (with, -se), to break into a passion (syn. ćiṛnā); to break loose from control. to revolt, rise (against), make an attack (upon); to turn wild and wanton, run riot; to get into disorder, run to ruin; to be perverse, refractory. obstinate, &c. ( = ḍhīṭh honā); to insist, persist (in).
s.m. A Brāhmaṇ, priest, poet or singer of Vedic hymns.
s.m. Adversity, calamity, misfortune, trouble, disaster, mishap; distress, pain, suffering
subs: (-par), Calamity or misfortune befalling, to be overtaken by calamity, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. Heroic, brave. mighty, powerful; — hero, brave man, warrior, champion (syn. bahādur); brother; a class of spirits employed by a magician to injure or molest anyone; supernatural power, spell, charm; a jewel worn in the ear; — s.f. Sister; tusk; pasturage, pasture (local)
subs: s.f. A man's own wife (used in North-Western India, particularly among the Jāṭs)
s.f. A silver or gold chain worn by women on the forehead

bīr-bahūṭī, bīr-bahoṭī [S. vīra+vadhuṭī], s.f. The red-velvet insect, the scarlet or lady-fly, Buccella carniola (commonly called the "rain-insect," as it makes its appearance when the first rains have fallen: it is covered with a downy exterior resembling velvet, and of a scarlet colour
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیر (bīr), from Middle Persian wyl (wīr), from Proto-Indo-European *wiHrós. Cognate with Avestan 𐬬𐬍𐬭𐬀 (vīra), Sanskrit वीर (vīra, “man (especially a brave one), chief, husband”), Northern Kurdish wêrek (“courageous”), Kashmiri ویٖر (vīr, “hero”), Ossetian ир (ir), Latin vir (“man”) Lithuanian výras, and English wer.

def: s.m. Power, strength; seed, &c. ( = bīj, q.v.).
s.m. Well, pit.
s.m. = bīṛā, q.v.
s.m. Brother ( = bīr, bīran).

s.m. A preparation of the areca nut with spices and chunam, &c. enveloped in a leaf of the betel plant (much used by the natives of India, and commonly presented by one to another in token of civility or affection. It is also given in confirmation of a pledge, promise, or betrothal; and, among the Rājpūts, is sometimes exchanged as a challenge); betel, pān (ten bīṛas = one gilauṛī); quid of tobacco; cheroot or cigar; — tie, fastening; thong tied to the hilt of a sword by which it is retained in the scabbard; sword-knot; hilt of a sword
subs: (-kā), To take up the gauntlet; to undertake to accomplish a business or affair; to take an enterprise upon oneself

(-kā), To propose a premium for the performance of a task (the custom was to throw a bīṛā into the midst of an assembly in token of an invitation to the undertaking of some difficult or hazardous enterprise, and the person who took up the bīṛā thereby bound himself to perform the task in question)
s.m. A bīṛā of seven betel leaves sent by the father of the bride to the bridegroom as a sign of betrothal.
ex: چین کا بیڑا اس وقت بحر الکاہل میں ہے۔
cīn kā beṛā is vaqt bahar-ul-kāhil mẽ hai.
China's fleet is currently in the Pacific Ocean.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀩𑁂𑀟𑀕 (beḍaga), from Sanskrit बेडक (beḍaka), बेडा (beḍā). Cognate to Sindhi ٻِيڙِي (ḇīṛī).

def: s.m. Making; compiling, composing, composition, compilation; embellishing, embellishment.
v.t. To make, fabricate, form, compose, &c. (see biraćan).
s.m. Fame, reputation, honour, glory; eulogy, panegyric.
s.m. Absence of desire or passion, indifference, disinclination, aversion; disregard of all sensual enjoyment; stoicism.
s.m. Absence of desire or passion, indifference, disinclination, aversion; disregard of all sensual enjoyment; stoicism.
adj. & s.m. Void of passion or desire; entertaining aversion or dislike; — stoical person, stoic; ascetic.
s.m. Separation (from, -kā or -se), parting, absence (particularly of lovers); love-song describing the pain of separation
subs: birah-kī mārī, adj. & s.m.f. Stricken with the pain of separation; one who is so stricken ( = birhan, and birogan, birogī).

def: (S. virah+agni), s.f. lit. 'The fire of separation'; the intense grief or sorrow which possesses a lover who is separated from his beloved.
s.f. A woman suffering the pangs of separation from the object of her affection; a female lover whose beloved one is absent ( = birogan).
adj. & s.m. Suffering the pangs of absence from a beloved one, love-sick; amorous, sensual; — one who is love-sick, &c.
s.f. = birahan, q.v. Sickness resulting from separation, love-sickness.
s.m. Splendour, beauty ( = birāṭ, and see virāj).
adj. Splendid, brilliant, shining, handsome, gorgeous; holding the sceptre, sitting in state, presiding.

v.t. To impart splendour or lustre to, to adorn, grace, beautify; — v.n. To shine, be splendid or conspicuous; to appear; to sit in state, sit majestically; to preside; to lord it, live at ease, enjoy oneself; to live in health, ease, content, or independence.
part. & s.m. Disinclined; inattentive; wearied, disgusted, alienated; — a kind of ascetic who has relinquished the world.
ex: اَچّھے کاموں سے بَرْکَتیں مِلْتی ہَیں۔
acche kāmõ se barkatẽ miltī ha͠i.
You receive blessings from doing good deeds.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian برکت (barakat), from Arabic بَرَكَة (baraka).

def: adj. & adv. Not close or compact, open in texture; thin, delicate, fine; loose, wide apart; apart, remote; rare, unfrequent, uncommon, scarce, wonderful; little, few; — rarely, seldom, unfrequently.

s.m. Cessation, rest, repose; stoppage, stop, pause; name of a small oblique stroke placed under a consonant to denote that it is quiescent, or that it has no vowel inherent or otherwise pronounced after it (syn. sukūn).
adj. corr. of bīmār, q.v.
corr. of bīmār, and of be-ārām, q.q.v.
v.n. To stop, pause; to remain; to delay (cf. bilambnā).
s.m. Brother ( = bīr, bīrā).
s.m. A kind of coarse, tough grass found covering uncultivated lands
adj. (f. -ī), Of, or relating to, or belonging to, another; another's; strange, foreign; extraneous, extrinsic (cf. P. bīrūnī).

ety: birā˚ = birāw˚ = Prk. विरावे(इ) or विराव(इ), fr. S. विराव
def: v.t. To vex, annoy, offend; to tease, mock, jibe, jeer.
adj. Ninety-two ( = bānwe, q.v.).
s.m. Name of Brahmā (also written birinć, birinći).
v.t. = birānā, To vex, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Of, or relating to, or belonging to, another; another's; strange, foreign; extraneous, extrinsic (cf. P. bīrūnī).
adj. Eighty-two = byāsī, q.v. adj. Eighty-two.
v.n. To stay, remain, stop, tarry, delay (syn. rahnā; ṭhaharnā; bilambnā).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑀭𑀺𑀲𑀇 (varisaï), from Sanskrit वर्षति (varṣati).

def: v.n. = bilasnā, q.v. v.n. To be pleased or delighted; to derive pleasure (from), take delight (in), to enjoy (oneself).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑀭𑀺𑀲𑀇 (varisaï), from Sanskrit वर्षति (varṣati).

def: Splendour, beauty; the embodied spirit.
s.m. Name of the province of Berar in Central India; name of an ancient king.
s.f. = bīr-bahuṭṭī, q.v.s.v. bīr.
s.f. = bīr-bahuṭṭī, q.v.s.v. bīr.
